work_experience_normalized.position: Developer of a Graphical User Interface for Engineering Software
work_experience_normalized.description: Early-stage development of a graphical user interface (GUI) for a computational model developed at the UPM, to analyze and design geothermal boreholes. Definition of main functions, elements, and distribution of the GUI. Programming of the first versions and iterations of the different modules of the GUI.

international_experience_normalized.institution_or_company: DB Systemtechnik GmbH - Section for Aerodynamics and Air Conditioning (Munich, Germany)
international_experience_normalized.description: Computational fluid dynamics (CFD) study for the enlargement of DBS climatic chamber in Münster, for weather-independent HVAC tests on railroad vehicles. Investigation of diverse inlet and outlet schemes and compliance with European norms on HVAC tests. Simulations carried out using Ansys CFX and optimized in cooperation with DBS HVAC laboratories. Final simulations validated new design, enabled definition of guidelines to circumvent potential problems, provided a model to study operational situations inside the chamber.
Studied for one year at RWTH Aachen.
